name: Inverted Row Between Chairs
shortDescription: A bodyweight pulling exercise performed using a bar or sturdy broomstick supported between two chairs.
instructions: Set up a sturdy bar or stick horizontally between two secure chairs. Lie on your back underneath the bar, grasp it with an overhand, shoulder-width grip, and keep your body straight from head to heels. Pull your chest toward the bar, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower yourself back down with control.
steps: Set up a sturdy bar between two heavy chairs.
Lie underneath with your chest directly below the bar.
Grip the bar with an overhand, shoulder-width grip.
Brace your body, keeping a straight line from head to heels.
Pull your chest to the bar, pause, then lower yourself slowly.
breathing: Exhale as you pull up, inhale as you lower down.
